title: SriLankan Airlines Adds First Wide-Body in Seven Years
dek: An Airbus A330-200 touching down in Colombo signals the flag carrier's push to rebuild capacity and reliability while a long-running restructuring continues.
body:
SriLankan Airlines welcomed an Airbus A330-200 to Bandaranaike International Airport (CMB) in early June 2025, reported as the carrier's first wide-body induction in more than seven years. For an airline that has spent recent years constrained by a tight balance sheet and an ageing, partly grounded fleet, the arrival is a tangible marker that capacity rebuilding is under way. The aircraft was added on a multi-year lease rather than an outright purchase, in keeping with the carrier's cautious, capital-light approach.

Capacity to match restored demand

The additional wide-body gives SriLankan more room to firm up frequencies on its core wide-body markets across South Asia, the Gulf and Southeast Asia, where Colombo functions as a natural connecting point over the Indian Ocean. Returning previously grounded jets to service and adding lift can ease the schedule reliability pressures that come with operating too few aircraft for an extended network.

Management has signalled intent to grow the fleet further over the coming years with a mix of wide-bodies and new-generation narrow-bodies, though delivery timing for any such plans remains subject to financing and market conditions.

Restructuring still sets the pace

The fleet move sits inside a broader, government-linked restructuring of the loss-making airline, including efforts to address legacy debt. The pace and shape of any ownership change will heavily influence how quickly SriLankan can modernise.

  • Hub: Bandaranaike International Airport, Colombo (CMB)
  • Aircraft type: Airbus A330-200, added on lease
  • Strategic role: South Asia, Gulf and Southeast Asia connectivity

For now, each additional airframe is a concrete step toward a more dependable network.
heroImageAlt: A twin-engine Airbus A330-200 wide-body parked on the apron at Colombo airport
